beginner.label: Beginner
beginner.description: New to incurred cost submissions? Covers what ICS is, who must file, provisional vs. final rates, the six schedules, and a first-timer end-to-end scenario.

modules.title: What Is an Incurred Cost Submission?
modules.description: Get a plain-English overview of what an Incurred Cost Submission is, why it exists, and how it fits into the government contract lifecycle.

modules.title: Who Must File and When
modules.description: Learn which contractors must file, what contract types trigger the requirement, and the consequences of missing the June 30 deadline.

modules.title: Provisional vs. Final Rates
modules.description: Understand the difference between provisional billing rates used during the year and the final rates computed after year-end.

modules.title: The Six Core Schedules
modules.description: Get a clear overview of Schedules A through H — what each one covers and how they connect to form a complete submission.

modules.title: Direct vs. Indirect Costs in ICS
modules.description: See how the direct and indirect cost classification from FAR Part 31 flows directly into your ICS schedules.

modules.title: Reconciling Claimed vs. Billed Costs
modules.description: Learn why overbillings and underbillings occur when provisional rates differ from final rates, and how to correct them.

modules.title: DCAA and the ICS
modules.description: Understand what DCAA looks for in a submission, what triggers an audit, and the basics of staying clean.

modules.title: Beginner End-to-End Scenario
modules.description: Follow a small 8(a) contractor through their first complete ICS filing from start to finish.

intermediate.label: Intermediate
intermediate.description: Comfortable with the basics? Go deeper on FAR 52.216-7, schedule mechanics, rate variances, unallowable cost segregation, and common DCAA deficiencies.

modules.title: ICS Regulatory Foundation
modules.description: Master FAR 52.216-7 — the Allowable Cost and Payment clause — and understand the full regulatory foundation for ICS filing requirements.

modules.title: Schedule Deep Dive: Schedule A
modules.description: Deep-dive into Schedule A, the summary of claimed indirect costs: structure, line items, and the most common errors DCAA flags.

modules.title: Schedule Deep Dive: Schedule B
modules.description: Examine Schedule B in detail — indirect expense pools and allocation bases — and see how it links back to your pool rate calculations.

modules.title: Schedule Deep Dive: Schedules C–H
modules.description: Work through Schedules C through H: direct costs by contract, contract listing, organizational chart, and the contractor certification.

modules.title: Rate Variances and Adjustments
modules.description: Learn how to compute the variance between provisional and final rates, determine over/under-billed amounts, and record adjustments.

modules.title: Unallowable Cost Identification in ICS
modules.description: Master the segregation of unallowable costs in the ICS — what must be excluded, how to document it, and what DCAA tests for.

modules.title: Common ICS Deficiencies
modules.description: Review the top reasons DCAA issues ICS deficiency letters and how to avoid each one before you submit.

modules.title: Intermediate Case Study
modules.description: Follow a mid-size contractor as it reconciles a full year of provisional billings across three indirect cost pools.

advanced.label: Advanced
advanced.description: Expert topics: multi-segment filings, CAS alignment, quick-close procedures, the full DCAA audit lifecycle, and ICS in M&A scenarios.

modules.title: Multi-Segment ICS Filings
modules.description: Learn how to structure ICS filings across multiple business segments or divisions with separate pools and allocation bases.

modules.title: CAS Disclosure Statement Alignment
modules.description: Ensure your ICS is consistent with your CAS Disclosure Statement — understand where the two frameworks interact and what triggers a CAS noncompliance finding.

modules.title: Quick-Close vs. Full ICS
modules.description: Determine when your organization qualifies for quick-close procedures and how to execute a quick-close ICS efficiently.

modules.title: DCAA Audit Process End-to-End
modules.description: Walk through the complete DCAA audit lifecycle after submission: desk review, sampling, field audit, preliminary findings, and exit conference.

modules.title: Responding to DCAA Findings
modules.description: Build a strategy for drafting rebuttals, supporting questioned costs, negotiating disallowances, and escalating to the Contracting Officer.

modules.title: Statute of Limitations and Final Decisions
modules.description: Understand the 6-year statute of limitations on ICS years, the finality of Contracting Officer determinations, and your appeal rights.

modules.title: ICS in M&A and Novation Scenarios
modules.description: Navigate what happens to open ICS years, pending audits, and rate negotiations during acquisitions, divestitures, and novation agreements.

modules.title: Advanced Case Study
modules.description: Apply every advanced concept as a large contractor navigates a DCAA audit, contested findings, and final rate negotiations.
